User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the eBikeNow app.

Admin.title: Admin
Admin.description: Represents an administrator of the eBikeNow app.

Bike.title: Bike
Bike.description: Represents an e-bike available for rent.

status.description: The current status of the bike (available, rented, locked).

station.description: The station where the bike is located.

Rental.title: Rental
Rental.description: Represents a rental record for an e-bike.

id.description: Unique identifier for the rental record.

bikeId.description: Reference to the Bike that was rented. (Relationship: Bike 1:N Rental)

userId.description: Reference to the User who rented the bike. (Relationship: User 1:N Rental)

startTime.description: The date and time the rental started.

endTime.description: The date and time the rental ended.  Null if the rental is active.

price.description: The total price of the rental.

definition.description: Stores user information.  Path-based ownership ensures only the user can modify their own document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Documents in this collection denote admin privileges. Existence of a document for a user ID grants admin rights.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who has admin privileges, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores information about each bike, including its status and station.

params.name: bikeId
params.description: The unique ID of the bike.

definition.description: Stores rental records. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, allowing rules to verify ownership without requiring 'get()' operations.

params.name: rentalId
params.description: The unique ID of the rental record.

definition.description: Stores user specific rentals. This duplicates some data to allow for easy listing of user-specific rentals.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: rentalId
params.description: The unique ID of the rental record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the eBikeNow app, focusing on security, scalability, and ease of querying. It leverages structural segregation and denormalization to ensure authorization independence and to enable efficient and secure data access.  The key collections are `users`, `bikes`, and `rentals`, with a separate `admins` collection for administrative roles.

Authorization Independence is achieved by:

*   For rentals, the `userId` is stored directly within the `/rentals/{rentalId}` document, enabling rules to verify ownership without needing to perform `get()` operations to the `/users/{userId}` collection.  This allows for atomic creation and updates of rentals.

The QAPs are supported as follows:

*   **List Bikes (Availability):** The `/bikes` collection allows listing all bikes, and rules can efficiently filter based on the `status` field (available, rented, locked) without complex logic.
*   **List User Rentals:** The `/users/{userId}/rentals/{rentalId}` structure allows a user to only list rentals under their user ID, ensuring data privacy and security.
*   **Admin Roles:** The `/roles_admin/{uid}` ensures that only listed admins can modify the bicycles.
